title: Germany World Cup 2026 Roster: Nagelsmann's Squad and Jamal Musiala Status
original_text:
Germany's World Cup 2026 squad: Nagelsmann's 26 and a bid to win it again
Julian Nagelsmann named Germany's 26-man World Cup 2026 squad on May 21, 2026, with captain Joshua Kimmich leading a group that blends elite young attackers with the surprise return of 40-year-old goalkeeper Manuel Neuer. Jamal Musiala is included after a serious injury, and Florian Wirtz arrives for his first World Cup. Below are all 26 players by position, Germany's Group E fixtures, the Musiala update, and where to back Die Mannschaft among our World Cup 2026 betting sites.
Last updated: June 4, 2026. This is Germany's final 26, confirmed ahead of the June 1 FIFA deadline. Tournament rules allow an injured player to be replaced up to 24 hours before the opener against Curaçao on June 14.
(Photo by Alex Grimm/Getty Images)
Germany World Cup 2026 roster TL;DR
Head coach: Julian Nagelsmann (in charge since September 2023)
Roster status: Final 26-man squad, announced May 21, 2026
Captain: Joshua Kimmich (Bayern Munich), with Antonio Rüdiger as vice-captain
Key player: Jamal Musiala (Bayern Munich), in the squad after returning from a serious injury
Group: Group E, with Curaçao, Ivory Coast and Ecuador
First match: June 14 vs Curaçao, NRG Stadium, Houston (1:00 PM ET)
Squad makeup: 3 goalkeepers, 8 defenders, 8 midfielders, 7 forwards
World Cup history: Four-time champions (1954, 1974, 1990, 2014), 21st appearance, group-stage exits in 2018 and 2022
Germany World Cup 2026 roster: full squad list
(Photo by Alex Grimm/Getty Images)
Nagelsmann built the squad around a Bayern Munich core of seven players, with most of the rest drawn from the Bundesliga and the Premier League. Position labels are approximate, since several of Germany's attackers are versatile, but the list below groups every player as named. The club each was attached to at selection is included.
PositionPlayerClubNote
Goalkeeper Manuel Neuer Bayern Munich First choice
Goalkeeper Oliver Baumann Hoffenheim
Goalkeeper Alexander Nübel Stuttgart
Defender Joshua Kimmich Bayern Munich Captain
Defender Antonio Rüdiger Real Madrid Vice-captain
Defender Jonathan Tah Bayern Munich
Defender Nico Schlotterbeck Borussia Dortmund
Defender Waldemar Anton Borussia Dortmund
Defender David Raum RB Leipzig
Defender Malick Thiaw Newcastle United
Defender Nathaniel Brown Eintracht Frankfurt
Midfielder Jamal Musiala Bayern Munich Returning from injury
Midfielder Florian Wirtz Liverpool
Midfielder Leon Goretzka Bayern Munich
Midfielder Aleksandar Pavlović Bayern Munich
Midfielder Angelo Stiller Stuttgart
Midfielder Felix Nmecha Borussia Dortmund
Midfielder Nadiem Amiri Mainz
Midfielder Pascal Groß Brighton & Hove Albion
Forward Kai Havertz Arsenal
Forward Leroy Sané Galatasaray
Forward Deniz Undav Stuttgart
Forward Nick Woltemade Newcastle United
Forward Maximilian Beier Borussia Dortmund
Forward Jamie Leweling Stuttgart
Forward Lennart Karl Bayern Munich Youngest, 18
Squad total: 26 (3 goalkeepers, 8 defenders, 8 midfielders, 7 forwards).
Is Jamal Musiala fit for Germany's World Cup 2026 squad?
Yes. Jamal Musiala is in the squad after recovering from the serious injury he suffered at the 2025 Club World Cup. He returned to club action with Bayern Munich during the season and went straight back into the national team, and the question now is sharpness rather than availability.
Nagelsmann has played down any concern, saying that even at 95 percent Musiala is one of the outstanding players in world football and that he has become more physically robust since the injury. The 23-year-old is Germany's most unpredictable attacker, and how quickly he reaches top form could shape how far the team goes.
He is not the only star carrying expectation. Florian Wirtz arrives for his first World Cup as one of the best attacking midfielders in the game, having joined Liverpool from Bayer Leverkusen, and he scored the opening goal of Euro 2024. The biggest headline of the squad announcement came in goal, where 40-year-old Manuel Neuer reversed his international retirement to be Nagelsmann's first choice for a fifth and final World Cup, with Oliver Baumann and Alexander Nübel as backups. This section will be updated as Musiala's fitness develops.
Who was left out of Germany's World Cup 2026 roster?
The biggest omission was winger Serge Gnabry, ruled out by injury. Up front, Nagelsmann left out Niclas Füllkrug, whose hold-up style reportedly did not fit the faster attacking shape the coach wants, while Karim Adeyemi and Kevin Schade missed out after inconsistent club seasons.
Germany also head to the tournament without several recent mainstays who have retired from international football since Euro 2024, including Thomas Müller, Toni Kroos and İlkay Gündoğan. That turnover, plus Neuer's return ahead of other goalkeeping options, has reshaped the spine of the team under Nagelsmann.
Germany World Cup 2026 fixtures
(Photo by Alexander Hassenstein/Getty Images)
Germany play across three host cities, including a Group E meeting with Ivory Coast at BMO Field in Toronto, the one game Canadian fans can catch in person.
DateOpponentVenueTime (ET)
Sun, June 14 Curaçao NRG Stadium, Houston 1:00 PM
Sat, June 20 Ivory Coast BMO Field, Toronto 4:00 PM
Thu, June 25 Ecuador MetLife Stadium, New Jersey 4:00 PM
All times Eastern. For the latest match prices as the group unfolds, our soccer odds page tracks Germany's lines.
Germany World Cup 2026 odds comparison
Germany sit in the next tier of contenders, behind the leading group of Spain, France, England, Brazil, Argentina and Portugal but well clear of the rest of the field. Most books price Die Mannschaft between 13.00 and 15.04 in decimal. The table below compares each sportsbook's price for Germany to lift the trophy. Outright odds move with form, injuries and results, so confirm the live price before betting, and see our World Cup 2026 betting sites guide for the full tournament market.

The short side is where the value disappears: Coolbet at 13.00 and 1xbet, BetLabel and 22bet at 13.20. On the outright, a winning $100 bet returns $1,504 at the top price against $1,300 at the shortest, so the books usually worth checking last are the ones pricing Germany highest here.
“Odds were captured simultaneously on June 2, 2026 at 8:00 AM ET and move constantly. Confirm the live price at the sportsbook before placing any bet. 19+ (18+ in Alberta, Manitoba and Quebec). Please play responsibly. ”
Editor's take: Germany's World Cup 2026 chances
Germany have the attacking talent to win this World Cup. A midfield built around Musiala and Wirtz, with Havertz ahead of them, can hurt anyone, and Group E should be a comfortable start. The problem is everything that has happened since 2014: two group-stage exits and a team still searching for the relentless identity that once defined it.
“Nagelsmann's boldest call is in goal, where 40-year-old Neuer returns from retirement. If his reflexes hold up and Musiala stays fit, I have Germany as a genuine semi-final side and a live outsider to go all the way. If the old doubts return at the first sign of pressure, they are also the kind of team that can go out early again. Talented enough to win it, fragile enough to fear, which is exactly why the odds sit where they do. ”
Sol Fayerman-HansenEditor-in-Chief
Roster status and changes
This is Germany's final 26. Under tournament rules, a player ruled out by a serious injury can be replaced up to 24 hours before the opener, so a late change remains possible if a fitness issue worsens.
The two storylines to watch are Musiala's sharpness as he builds back from injury and how Neuer performs at 40 after time away from international football. Germany trained at their base in Herzogenaurach before travelling to North America. Any official change will be reflected here, in the player table and in the FAQ.

FAQs on Germany at World Cup 2026
Common Questions on Germany at the World Cup
Who is in Germany's World Cup 2026 squad?
Julian Nagelsmann named 26 players. The headline names are captain Joshua Kimmich (Bayern Munich), Jamal Musiala (Bayern Munich), Florian Wirtz (Liverpool) and the returning goalkeeper Manuel Neuer (Bayern Munich). Seven of the 26 are Bayern Munich players. The full list by position is in the table above.
Is Jamal Musiala fit for the World Cup?
Yes. Musiala is in the squad after recovering from a serious injury suffered at the 2025 Club World Cup. He returned to club action with Bayern Munich late in the season and went straight back into the national team. Nagelsmann said even at 95 percent he is one of the best players in the world, so the question is sharpness rather than availability.
Why did Manuel Neuer return for Germany?
Neuer, 40, reversed his 2024 retirement from international football to compete at what is expected to be his fifth and final World Cup, and Nagelsmann confirmed he will be the first-choice goalkeeper. It was the biggest surprise of the squad announcement, with Oliver Baumann and Alexander Nübel named as the backup keepers.
Who is Germany's head coach at World Cup 2026?
Julian Nagelsmann, who took charge of Germany in September 2023. He led the team to the quarterfinals of Euro 2024 on home soil, where they lost to eventual champions Spain. He named the 26-man squad on May 21, 2026, as Germany chase a first World Cup title since 2014.
What group is Germany in at World Cup 2026?
Germany is in Group E with Curaçao, Ivory Coast and Ecuador. They play in Houston and New Jersey in the United States and in Toronto in Canada. The top two teams in each group advance to the round of 32, along with the eight best third-placed teams across the 48-nation tournament.
When is Germany's first World Cup 2026 match?
Germany opens against Curaçao on Sunday, June 14, 2026 at NRG Stadium in Houston, kickoff 1:00 PM ET. They then face Ivory Coast on June 20 in Toronto and Ecuador on June 25 in New Jersey. Germany are four-time world champions, last lifting the trophy in 2014.
